Title: Third DWF1 paralog in Solanaceae, sterol Δ24-isomerase, branches withanolide biosynthesis from the general phytosterol pathway
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Withanolide biosynthesis branching from general phytosterol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Branch point from 24-methylenecholesterol toward withanolide precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committing step; key branching enzyme for withanolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes the sterol side-chain double bond of 24-methylenecholesterol from Δ24(28) to Δ24(25), producing 24-methyldesmosterol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor and commits flux toward withanolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point; committed step; upstream entry to specialized pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DWF1/SSR family; FAD/FMN-binding oxidoreductase-like protein with N-terminal membrane anchor and Rossmann fold
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression correlates with 24-methyldesmosterol accumulation across tissues; in W. somnifera detected in aerial tissues analyzed; in C. annuum exclusive to roots; not detected for Sm24ISO in analyzed tissues
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Up-regulation or engineering of 24ISO could channel more substrate into withanolide biosynthesis for sustainable production of pharmacologically active withanolides; useful entry-point gene for pathway reconstruction and stacking with downstream tailoring enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Withania somnifera; Physalis alkekengi; Petunia hybrida; Capsicum annuum
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Withania somnifera; Physalis alkekengi; Petunia hybrida; Capsicum annuum
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Withania somnifera; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Withania somnifera; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Withania somnifera unspecified; Nicotiana benthamiana unspecified; yeast strains T21, T31, erg4
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: P. alkekengi var. Franchetii; W. somnifera unspecified; P. hybrida inbred line V26; C. annuum var. Tachiyatsufusa
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Ca24ISO: Capana06g002437; Sm24ISO/SSR-like: SMEL_008g315450; other accession numbers in SI Appendix Table S1/Dataset S1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacological activities including anti-inflammatory and potential treatment of cancer, arthritis, autoimmune and neurodegenerative diseases; defense compounds against insects
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves/aerial tissues in W. somnifera for withaferin A; tissue distribution varies among species
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of 24-methyldesmosterol and is required for withanolide biosynthesis; silencing reduces withaferin A and increases 24-methylenecholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 24ISO is a third DWF1 paralog in Solanaceae and correlates with occurrence of withanolides/petuniasterones. 2. Heterologous expression in yeast and N. benthamiana showed 24ISO converts 24-methylenecholesterol to 24-methyldesmosterol. 3. VIGS of Ws24ISO in W. somnifera reduced 24-methyldesmosterol and withaferin A, demonstrating that 24ISO is indispensable and likely the committing step for withanolide biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: 24ISO is absent, pseudogenized, or weakly expressed in several domesticated Solanum crops, suggesting possible selection against this branch during domestication; altered flux may compete with cholesterol-derived specialized metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; tissue qPCR-expression/metabolite correlation; heterologous expression in yeast with GC-MS; in vitro enzyme assay with NADPH requirement; transient overexpression in N. benthamiana; ER localization by confocal microscopy; VIGS in W. somnifera with GC-MS and LC-QTOF-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome reanalysis of W. somnifera SRA053485 and SRP040231; previously published P. alkekengi and P. peruviana transcriptome datasets; metabolomics/sterol profiling by GC-MS; LC-QTOF-MS for withanolides
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants grown in soil at 22°C under 16 h light/8 h dark; VIGS treatment in young W. somnifera; transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana; in vitro activity requires NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ws24ISO, Pa24ISO, Ph24ISO, and Ca24ISO show tissue-specific expression correlating with 24-methyldesmosterol; Ca24ISO root-specific; Sm24ISO not detected and likely pseudogene
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 24ISO evolved from SSR2 after duplication and retained weak Δ24(25)-reductase activity plus NADPH dependence despite no net redox change; 24ISO/SSR-like presence correlates with withanolide-producing lineages and petuniasterones in Petunia.
